name: Page
description: A page is a complete, published document accessible under a unique URL. It represents a full piece of communication intended for end-users. A page must have one clear purpose and follow editorial, structural, and information architecture (IA) rules. Every page has a fixed position of navigation on the top and footer on the bottom. All the page content is placed in between.
content:
## Composition model

Every page must include:  

| MANDATORY METADATA | DESCRIPTION |

| :---- | :---- |

| Page title (H1) | Maximum 60 characters |

| Meta description | Maximum 160 characters |

| Page URL | Clean, tight URLs, kebab-case path |

| Navigation | Must contain a path of navigation and indicate navigation level for the page: meganav, bubble or link from another page. |

| OpenGraph image | Indicate link to the page thumbnail, otherwise default image is used. |

  

A Page is structured into three mandatory vertical zones that appear in a fixed order: **Navigation → Content → Footer**. They appear consistently throughout the sites. Each of them has an individual structural role, restrictions and predefined behavior.

  

| AREA | POSITION | CONTAINS |

| :---- | :---- | :---- |

| Navigation | Top of the page | Navigation / Primary branding / Search |

| Content | Between header and footer | Content |

| Footer | Bottom of the page | Global utilities |

  

![Page.png](images/f30936cd-a723-42cf-af36-808904c18959.png)

appliedRules.rule: Prioritise critical information at the top. All users see the top of the page; only a minority reach the bottom. Ensure that the most important message, value proposition, or action is communicated immediately.
Follow the Copy style guide. For detailed rules of content execution, follow the official Canonical Copy style guide.
One place per concept. Each topic should have a single authoritative page. Do not create new pages where updates to existing pages are appropriate.
No orphan pages. All content must be reachable through the defined IA. Pages without a home will surface through search engines and degrade user trust.
No “coming soon” placeholders. Pages must ship completely or not at all. Avoid publishing stubs or speculative destinations.
Strengthen offers and calls to action. Ensure CTAs and lead-generation elements are compelling, clear, and optimised for conversion.
Use takeovers strategically. When implementing homepage or section takeovers, prioritise craft, wit, and visual quality. They should always lead somewhere meaningful.
Keep it lean. Design, copy, and IA should always move towards simplicity, reduction, and clarity.
Sites’ system uses predefined spacing applied to the page as a whole as well as to particular sections.
Number of CTAs in the layout should be as limited as possible.
Each page is defined by a unique H1 title (max 60 characters) and a meta description (max 160 characters) that establishes its context within the site.
Each page should have and OpenGraph image thumbnail, otherwise fallback default image is used.
